frameworks.name: decision under risk vs ambiguity
frameworks.one_line: Distinguishes known-probability risk from missing-information ambiguity in choice.

frameworks.name: expected utility / decision theory
frameworks.one_line: Predicts ambiguity should not affect choices — paper shows it does.

frameworks.name: lesion-deficit neuropsychology
frameworks.one_line: Tests causal role of orbitofrontal cortex via lesion patients.

core_question: Does the brain treat ambiguity differently from risk during human decision-making?
key_assumptions: Ambiguity is operationalised as missing information about probabilities
Decision theory predicts no ambiguity effect on choice
fMRI BOLD indexes valuation-related neural activity
manipulations: Level of ambiguity in choice options
Level of risk (known probability) in choice options
key_findings: Ambiguity level correlates positively with amygdala and orbitofrontal cortex activity
Ambiguity correlates negatively with a striatal system
Striatal activity correlates positively with expected reward
Orbitofrontal-lesion patients show insensitivity to ambiguity and risk level
interpretations: A general neural circuit responds to degrees of uncertainty across risk and ambiguity
Findings run contrary to decision-theoretic indifference to ambiguity
